summary: Liu Bei; besieged at Xiaopei by Ji Ling's army; sends for Lu Bu's help. After Lu Bu's halberd-shot trick lifts the siege, Liu Bei is forced into the awkward coexistence with Lu Bu that defines the next stretch.
Ji Ling; Yuan Shu's general besieging Xiaopei with 100,000 troops; called off when Lu Bu shoots his halberd at 150 paces from the camp gate.

Yuan Shu; ferries 100,000 carts of millet to Lu Bu to keep him neutral while crushing Liu Bei; foiled by the halberd shot, then loses again at Yangcheng. Pushes harder toward the imperial declaration.

Marches against Zhang Xiu at Wancheng — accepts the formal surrender, takes Zhang Xiu's aunt-by-marriage Lady Zou into his tent on the same night, and triggers Zhang Xiu's deadly revolt with Jia Xu's help. Loses his eldest son Cao Ang, his nephew Cao Anmin, and Dian Wei in the disaster.
Dian Wei dies at Wancheng — bare-chested, halberd snapped, holding the camp gate with corpses piled around him so Cao Cao can escape. The novel's defining bodyguard death.
Cao Ang; Cao Cao's eldest son; gives up his own horse to the fleeing Cao Cao and is killed by Zhang Xiu's troops at Wancheng. Cao Cao mourns him by name for years.
Lady Cao; named in passing in the Wancheng chapter as Cao Ang's mother — her grief over Cao Ang's death drives the rift with Cao Cao.

Zhang Xiu; nephew of Zhang Ji; surrenders Wancheng to Cao Cao, then revolts after Cao Cao takes his aunt as a concubine. With Jia Xu's plan, ambushes Cao Cao and inflicts the worst defeat of his early career.
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Jia Xu; now adviser to Zhang Xiu; designs the Wancheng revolt that kills Dian Wei, Cao Ang, and Cao Anmin. His reputation as the era's most ruthless strategist begins here.
Jia Xu (duplicate record).
Zhang Ji; killed in the chapter's opening — Cao Cao acquires Wancheng after his death, and Zhang Xiu (his nephew) succeeds him.
Zhang Ji (duplicate record).
Zhang Ji (duplicate record).
Zhang Ji (duplicate record).
Zhang Ji (duplicate record).
Lady Yan (often called Lady Zou in other translations); Zhang Ji's widow; taken into Cao Cao's tent at Wancheng. The provocation that triggers Zhang Xiu's revolt.
Guan Yu; with Liu Bei at Xiaopei during the Ji Ling siege.
Zhang Fei; with Liu Bei at Xiaopei during the Ji Ling siege.
Han Yin; Yuan Shu's envoy to Lu Bu with promises of gifts and a marriage alliance. Lu Bu cuts off the bride's marriage when Chen Gui talks him out of it.
Chen Gui; Chen Deng's father; argues Lu Bu out of marrying his daughter into the Yuan Shu clan — the strategic move that keeps Lu Bu isolated from a Yuan alliance.
Chen Deng; works with his father Chen Gui to set Lu Bu and Yuan Shu against each other; quietly aligning the Chen family with Cao Cao.
Li Jue; named in passing in the imperial recap.
Cheng Yu; advises Cao Cao through the Wancheng campaign.
Cheng Yu (duplicate record).
Guo Jia; with Cao Cao at Wancheng.
Xun Yu; running the Xuchang administration during the Wancheng campaign.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xu Chu; named in the chapter as Cao Cao's bodyguard captain (with Dian Wei still alive at the start).
Xiahou Dun; serves under Cao Cao in the Wancheng campaign.
Yu Jin; serves under Cao Cao in the Wancheng campaign.
Li Dian; serves under Cao Cao.
Gao Shun; named in Lu Bu's command structure.
Zhang Liao; named in Lu Bu's command structure.
Song Xian; named in Lu Bu's command structure.
Wei Xu; named in Lu Bu's command structure.
Cao Bao; named in the chapter recap of the earlier Xuzhou raid.
Chen Gong; adviser to Lu Bu through the chapter.
Chen Gong (duplicate record).
Chen Lan; minor figure named in passing.
Mi Zhu; named in passing as Liu Bei's adviser.
Sun Qian; named in passing as Liu Bei's adviser.
Sun Qian (duplicate record).
Liu Biao; named in passing in the regional roll.
Sun Ce; named in passing — south stable under his rule.
Yuan Shao; named in passing in the regional roll.
Yuan Shao (duplicate record).
Zhang Lu; named in passing as the Hanzhong Daoist ruler.
Liu Zhang; named in passing as Imperial Protector of Yi Province.
Wang Ze; minor figure named in passing.
Wang Ze (duplicate record).

Yuan Shu; declares himself emperor of 'Zhong/Cheng' on the strength of the imperial seal Sun Ce pledged. Sets out a court, names an empress, and dispatches seven armies under Zhang Xun against Liu Bei — provoking the four-way coalition (Cao Cao, Liu Bei, Lu Bu, Sun Ce) that crushes him.

Leads the central army against Yuan Shu's self-proclamation; in the famine that follows the campaign, executes his quartermaster Wang Hou with the line 'I borrow your head to quiet the army' — the chapter's most-cited example of his ruthlessness.
Zhang Xun; commander of Yuan Shu's seven armies; defeated and killed in the coalition campaign.
Ji Ling; serves under Zhang Xun in the seven-army host; killed by Zhang Fei in the fighting.
Liang Gang; one of Yuan Shu's named generals; killed in the campaign.
Lei Xu; one of Yuan Shu's named generals; killed in the campaign.
Chen Lan; one of Yuan Shu's named generals.
Qiao Rui; one of Yuan Shu's named generals.
Yue Jiu; one of Yuan Shu's named generals.
Yan Xiang; Yuan Shu's chief minister who opposes the imperial declaration and is silenced.
Yan Xiang (duplicate record).
Feng Fang; Yuan Shu's daughter is named as empress in the imperial declaration.
Liu Bei; joins the coalition against Yuan Shu, fighting alongside Lu Bu's army.
Guan Yu; rides with Liu Bei against Yuan Shu.
Chen Deng; coordinates between Lu Bu and the Cao Cao camp during the campaign.
Chen Gui; with his son Chen Deng quietly aligns Lu Bu's territory with the coalition.
Chen Gong; adviser to Lu Bu through the campaign.
Chen Gong (duplicate record).
Gao Shun; serves under Lu Bu.
Zhang Liao; serves under Lu Bu.
Zang Ba; serves under Lu Bu.
Song Xian; serves under Lu Bu.
Wei Xu; serves under Lu Bu.
Sun Ce; the southern arm of the coalition against Yuan Shu; recovers the territories Yuan Shu took along the river.
Zhang Zhao; Sun Ce's senior civil officer through the southern campaign.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Duan Wei; the new Imperial Protector of Liang Province; joins the coalition as the western arm against Yuan Shu's allies.
Wu Xi; one of Yuan Shu's named officers.
Cao Hong; serves under Cao Cao in the central army.
Cao Hong (duplicate record).
Cao Ren; serves under Cao Cao in the central army.
Xiahou Dun; serves under Cao Cao.
Dian Wei; named in passing in the chapter's recap — already dead at Wancheng.
Dian Man; Dian Wei's son, brought to Cao Cao who raises him in honor of his father's death.
Xu Chu; serves under Cao Cao in the central army.
Guo Jia; advises Cao Cao.
Yu Jin; serves under Cao Cao.
Li Jue; named in passing.
Guo Si; named in passing.
Han Yin; named in passing in the Yuan Shu recap.
Jia Xu; named in passing — adviser to Zhang Xiu.
Jia Xu (duplicate record).
Jin Shang; minor figure named in passing.
Yang Feng; defects from Yuan Shu's camp during the campaign.
Yang Feng (duplicate record).
Yang Feng (duplicate record).
Han Xian; defects with Yang Feng.
Chen Ji; minor figure named in passing.
Chen Ji (duplicate record).
Chen Ji (duplicate record).
Kuai Yue; named in passing in the Jingzhou roll.
Liu Biao; named in passing — refuses to commit to the coalition.
Ren Jun; supervises Cao Cao's farm-colony grain to feed the army through the chapter's famine.
Xun Yu; running Xuchang's administration.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xian; minor figure named in passing.
Zhang Xian (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu; named in passing — Cao Cao will return to deal with him later.
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).

Returns to deal with Zhang Xiu at Wancheng; falls for Jia Xu's second trick (the fake retreat that lures Cao Cao into a textbook ambush) and is forced to withdraw again. Returns to Xuchang and turns toward the Lu Bu problem at Xiapi.

Jia Xu; reads Cao Cao's troop dispositions, predicts the night assault, and counter-ambushes him — the second time he beats Cao Cao in two campaigns. Cements his reputation as the era's most dangerous adviser.
Jia Xu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu; with Jia Xu's plan ambushes Cao Cao a second time at Wancheng. Eventually surrenders to Cao Cao in chapter 23.
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).

Xiahou Dun; in this chapter's defining moment loses his left eye to Cao Xing's arrow at Xiapi — pulls the arrow out with the eyeball, shouts 'essence of my father, blood of my mother — I cannot waste it!' and swallows the eye before killing Cao Xing in revenge.
Cao Xing; Lu Bu's general; shoots Xiahou Dun in the eye with a bow at Xiapi, then is run through by Xiahou Dun's spear in the moments after.
Cao Xing (duplicate record).
Xiahou Yuan; carries his blinded brother off the field after the Cao Xing arrow strike.
Liu Bei; defeated at Xiaopei by Lu Bu's assault under Gao Shun and Zhang Liao; flees with Sun Qian to Cao Cao's camp.
Guan Yu; defends Xiaopei with Liu Bei; forced to break out when Gao Shun overruns the city.
Zhang Fei; defends Xiaopei alongside Liu Bei and Guan Yu.
Gao Shun; Lu Bu's general; takes Xiaopei from Liu Bei in this chapter.
Zhang Liao; serves under Lu Bu / Gao Shun in the Xiaopei assault.
Chen Gong; adviser to Lu Bu; encourages the Xiaopei attack.
Chen Gong (duplicate record).
Chen Deng; with his father, works inside Lu Bu's camp as Cao Cao's double agent.
Chen Gui; with Chen Deng works as Cao Cao's double agent inside Lu Bu's camp.
Mi Zhu; with Liu Bei in the flight from Xiaopei.
Mi Fang; Mi Zhu's brother; flees Xiaopei with Liu Bei.
Sun Qian; with Liu Bei in the flight.
Sun Qian (duplicate record).
Jian Yong; named with Liu Bei's group during the flight.
Cao Ang; named in passing in the chapter's recap — already dead at Wancheng.
Dian Wei; named in passing in the recap.
Li Dian; serves under Cao Cao.
Yu Jin; serves under Cao Cao.
Guo Jia; advises Cao Cao.
Xun Yu; running Xuchang.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun You; with Cao Cao.
Hou Cheng; serves under Lu Bu.
Song Xian; serves under Lu Bu.
Wei Xu; serves under Lu Bu.
Zang Ba; serves under Lu Bu.
Sun Guan; one of Lu Bu's eight named generals.
Wu Dun; one of Lu Bu's eight named generals.
Yin Li; one of Lu Bu's eight named generals.
Yin Li (duplicate record).
Chang Xi; minor figure named in passing.
Li Tong; defects to Cao Cao with his family troops.
Li Tong (duplicate record).
Liu Biao; named in passing in the regional roll.
Kuai Liang; named in passing in the Jingzhou roll.
Huang Zu; named in passing in the Jingzhou roll.
Sun Ce; named in passing in the regional roll.
Gongsun Zan; named in passing in the regional roll.
Yuan Shao; named in passing in the regional roll.
Yuan Shao (duplicate record).
Yuan Shu; named in passing — his self-proclamation has collapsed.

Closes the Lu Bu account: floods Xiapi from the rivers Yi and Si, takes the city when Hou Cheng, Song Xian, and Wei Xu defect from within. Receives the bound Lu Bu, hears him plead for life, and on Liu Bei's reminder of Ding Yuan and Dong Zhuo has him strangled at the White Gate Tower. Takes in Zhang Liao with Guan Yu's intercession.
Chen Gong; refuses any plea bargain on the White Gate Tower scaffold — kneels, asks only that his elderly mother be cared for, and walks calmly to his execution. Cao Cao honors the request.
Chen Gong (duplicate record).

Zhang Liao; spits at Cao Cao on the scaffold; Guan Yu kneels to intercede and Liu Bei joins him; Cao Cao laughs, frees him, and takes him into service — one of the chapter's defining recruitments. Will become the hero of Hefei.
Hou Cheng; one of the three Lu Bu officers who betray him — steals the halberd while Lu Bu sleeps. Spared by Cao Cao.
Song Xian; ties up the sleeping Lu Bu and presents him to Cao Cao. Spared.
Wei Xu; with Hou Cheng and Song Xian, betrays Lu Bu from inside Xiapi. Spared by Cao Cao.
Gao Shun; refuses to renounce Lu Bu; executed alongside him at the White Gate Tower.
Lady Yan; Lu Bu's wife; pleads tearfully with him not to lead a sortie. Her tears keep him in the city long enough for his officers to betray him.
Liu Bei; rides with Cao Cao to Xiapi. On the scaffold, reminds Cao Cao 'remember Ding Yuan and Dong Zhuo' — the line that seals Lu Bu's death sentence.
Guan Yu; kneels before Cao Cao on the scaffold to spare Zhang Liao — the recruitment that gives Wei one of its greatest generals.
Zhang Fei; rides with Liu Bei at Xiapi.
Cao Ren; serves under Cao Cao at the Xiapi siege.
Cheng Yu; advises Cao Cao at the siege.
Cheng Yu (duplicate record).
Guo Jia; advises Cao Cao to flood the city when conventional siege stalls.
Xu Chu; with Cao Cao at the siege.
Xiahou Dun; serves at the siege despite his recent eye wound.
Xiahou Yuan; serves at the siege.
Xun You; with Cao Cao at the siege.
Xun Yu; running Xuchang.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xu Huang; with Cao Cao at the siege.
Mi Zhu; with Liu Bei.
Sun Qian; with Liu Bei.
Sun Qian (duplicate record).
Jian Yong; with Liu Bei.
Sun Guan; one of Lu Bu's eight named generals; surrenders with the city.
Wu Dun; one of Lu Bu's eight named generals.
Yin Li; one of Lu Bu's eight named generals.
Yin Li (duplicate record).
Chang Xi; mountain bandit chief approached for help during the siege; refuses.
Chen Deng; the Chen family delivers Xiapi's outer gates to Cao Cao from inside.
Chen Gui; with Chen Deng helps deliver Xiapi to Cao Cao.
Xu Si; minor figure named in passing.
Yang Chou; minor figure named in passing.
Shi Huan; minor figure named in passing.
Wang Kai; minor figure named in passing.
Wang Kai (duplicate record).
Wang Kai (duplicate record).
Pang Shu; minor figure named in passing.
Liu Biao; named in passing in the regional roll.
Yuan Shu; named in passing — his collapse is accelerating.
Yuan Shao; named in passing as the great power to the north.
Yuan Shao (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu; named in passing.
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Yang; named in passing — Henei lord.
Ding Yuan; named in Liu Bei's reminder line on the scaffold — the man Lu Bu killed to take Red Hare.
Dong Zhuo; named alongside Ding Yuan in Liu Bei's reminder on the scaffold.

Holds the great hunt at Xutian in the emperor's presence; takes the imperial bow and shoots the deer, then accepts the soldiers' cries of 'Long live the emperor!' as if for himself. The arrogance that triggers the belt-decree conspiracy.

Liu Bei; restrains Guan Yu from killing Cao Cao on the hunting field — survival, not loyalty; he is now an imperial uncle by recognition. Recruited by Dong Cheng into the belt-decree conspiracy by the chapter's end.
Guan Yu; spurs forward at the hunt with his blade ready to cut Cao Cao down; Liu Bei stops him with a glance. The 'imperial uncle' chapter.
Emperor Xian; humiliated when Cao Cao takes the imperial bow at the hunt; gives Dong Cheng the secret belt-decree in his palace robe with a finger pierced for blood. The set piece of the chapter.
Emperor Xian (duplicate record).
Emperor Xian (duplicate record).
Emperor Xian (duplicate record).
Emperor Xian (duplicate record).
Dong Cheng; receives the blood-written belt-decree from Emperor Xian, hides it in the seam of the robe, and begins recruiting the conspirators. The chapter's central figure.
Ma Teng's debut at court — visits Xuchang and joins Dong Cheng's belt-decree conspiracy as the first named co-signatory after Liu Bei.
Wang Zifu; co-signatory to the belt-decree pledge.
Wu Zilan; co-signatory to the belt-decree pledge.
Yang Biao; co-signatory to the belt-decree pledge.
Che Zhou; appointed Governor of Xuzhou under Cao Cao when Liu Bei nominally departs for the area.
Kong Rong; at court in Xuchang; takes part in the recap of recent regional shifts.
Cheng Yu; with Cao Cao at the hunt.
Cheng Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu; in Xuchang.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Fu Wan; Empress Fu's father; conspires with the emperor's circle around the belt-decree.
Shen Heng; minor official named in passing.
Liu Xiong; minor imperial-clan figure named in passing.
Chang Xi; named in passing in the regional roll.
Guo Si; named in passing — already dead by this point in the timeline.
Li Jue; named in passing — already dead.
Dong Zhuo; named in passing in the chapter's recap.
Yuan Shu; named in passing — his collapse continues offstage.
Zang Ba; named in passing.
Zhang Liao; named in passing — now in Cao Cao's service.
Zhang Liang; opening-frame reference to the Han-founding strategist, in a passage about loyal counsel.
Zhang Liang (duplicate record).
Zhao Yan; minor figure named in passing.
